entities.name: P2 (SSOT)
entities.context: Single Source of Truth principle - reference protocol as single source, don't duplicate

entities.name: P5 (Anti-Overwrite)
entities.context: Safety principle - check for conflicts before creating files

entities.name: P7 (Dry-Run)
entities.context: Quality principle - all digests must support --dry-run flag

entities.name: P11 (Failure Modes)
entities.context: Safety principle - document recovery for common errors

entities.name: P19 (Error Handling)
entities.context: Quality principle - all scripts have try-catch with logging

entities.name: P1 (Human-Readable)
entities.context: Quality principle - markdown output, clear sections

entities.name: P16 (Accuracy)
entities.context: Quality principle - no invented limitations, cite real constraints

entities.name: P18 (State Verification)
entities.context: Quality principle - always verify file writes succeeded

entities.name: P21 (Document Assumptions)
entities.context: Design principle - design doc captures all assumptions

entities.name: P8 (Minimal Context)
entities.context: Design principle - load only what's needed per phase

entities.name: Recovery
entities.context: Process for identifying missing dependencies, updating script/docs

entities.name: Deployment Phase
entities.context: Phase in development lifecycle where scheduled tasks run

entities.name: Scheduled task
entities.context: Automated job that can fail on first run, requires log checking

relationships.context: SSOT principle guides recovery by referencing protocol as single source
Both relate to documentation and knowledge organization
Safety principles that prevent and verify file operations
Both prepare for and handle operational failures
Failure Modes documentation enables Recovery processes
Failure Modes apply to common Deployment Phase errors
Error Handling with logging supports Failure Mode documentation
Define completeness before claiming deployment success
Accuracy requires documented assumptions with real constraints
Minimal context loading enables targeted recovery operations
